compute.cost_note: Per-chapter cost is variance_audit's: Tier 1 is stdlib + tokenization; Tier 2/3 need spaCy + en_core_web_sm; runs all chapters, so cost scales with chapter count (a chapter audit cache supports resume on long runs).

do_not_use_when: You want a single-chapter or short-passage diagnosis (use variance_audit directly)
You want an authorship verdict — the dashboard reports per-chapter band labels, not a label; aggregation uses medians, not a verdict
The chapters are ESL writing without a matched ESL baseline (inherits variance_audit's ESL polarity caveat)

examples.description: Manuscript-wide Layer A dashboard against a baseline

purpose: Cross-chapter aggregate of the Layer A distributional diagnostic. Runs variance_audit's signal machinery (audit_text / classify_compression) on every chapter of a manuscript (or every file in a chapter directory) and reports a dashboard of per-signal medians, per-chapter band labels, and outlier chapters that single-chapter audits miss.
use_when: You want manuscript-wide distributional evidence of mode-collapse and which chapters deviate from baseline
You have a multi-chapter manuscript (or a chapter directory) and an optional baseline corpus for z-score comparison
Each chapter clears the Tier 1 length floor (≥ 200 words); thin chapters degrade the per-chapter estimate
